author: kevaundray

author: taulepton_
content:
<@466102668138512394> it looks like the reth-zisk guest is returning a public input that does not match the expected public input and thats resulting in some verification issues downstream when proofs are gossiped. Do you do any internal consistency checking of the public input? 
I'm using:
- ghcr.io/eth-act/zkboost/zkboost-server:0.3.0
- ghcr.io/eth-act/ere/ere-server-zisk:0.6.1-cuda
- https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.7.0/stateless-validator-reth-zisk

author: jsign
content:
Uhm, yeah we have ~25 mainnet blocks integration tests in ere-guest CI, so that sounds wierd. Also, the calculation is pretty much [hash_tree_root of whatever is received as input](https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/blob/main/crates/stateless-validator-reth/src/guest.rs#L57). 
Is this mismatch maybe related to output length? Because there is a quirk that I commented [here](https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/pull/27#discussion_r2975736758) reg how Zisk works today that might nto be obvious. i.e. Zisk pads the public input to 256 bytes so doesn't fully respect exactly what the guest program wrote

author: jsign
content: If the public input length isn't the prob, maybe also worth checking if libssz which is the crate we use in the guest program matches the same hash_tree_root of whatever the CL is using (I assume ethereum_ssz maybe).

author: han0110
content: Ah the public values padding of `zisk@0.16.0` is probably the issue, will fix that soon

author: taulepton_
content: thanks for the context, that makes sense
